title: Carnival's CEO to step down in August, to become vice chair
text: Carnival PLC (LSE:CCL) and Carnival Corporation (NYSE:CCL) chief executive Arnold Donald will step down in August and be replaced by the company's chief operations officer, Josh Weinstein. Donald, one of relatively few black chief executives at major US companies, has led the cruise line operator since 2013 and will continue on the board as vice chairman.

title: Carnival CEO Arnold Donald steps down as cruise industry aims for a refresh

title: Carnival Corp. names replacement for CEO Arnold Donald

title: Carnival Names Josh Weinstein To Assume CEO Role
text: Carnival Corp (NYSE: CCL) has appointed its Chief Operations Officer Josh Weinstein to assume the role of President and CEO, effective August 1, 2022. Arnold Donald, currently President and CEO, will take up the role of Vice-Chair and member of the Board.

title: Carnival CEO Arnold Donald to Step Down in August as Pandemic Rebound Continues
text: The cruise operator taps operating chief, Josh Weinstein, to succeed one of the few African-Americans to lead a large U.S. company.

title: Carnival names operations head Weinstein as next CEO of cruise operator
text: (Reuters) -Carnival Corp the world's largest cruise company, on Tuesday said Josh Weinstein, its chief operations officer, would become the next chief executive officer, taking over the role from Arnold Donald.

title: Carnival names new CEO effective August 1
text: Carnival Corp. CCL, -2.38% CUK, -1.92% has named 48-year-old Josh Weinstein as chief executive of the company, succeeding Arnold Donald, effective August 1. Donald will become vice chair and a member of the cruise company's board.

title: Harland & Wolff wins dry docking contract from P&O Cruises and Cunard
text: Harland & Wolff Group Holdings PLC (AIM:HARL)'s subsidiary, Harland & Wolff (Belfast) Limited has been awarded a contract to dry dock two cruise ships from Carnival Corporation (NYSE:CCL) subsidiaries P&O Cruises and Cunard. The two ships - P&O Cruises' Aurora and Cunard's Queen Victoria - will stay in the Belfast shipyard for 33 days in total, where they will undergo standard drydocking operations, allowing Harland & Wolff to demonstrate its capabilities and expertise in this kind of projects to the wider cruise industry.

title: Carnival Corp. stock gains nearly 3% after cruise operator has busiest booking week ever
text: Shares of Carnival Corp. CCL, -1.55% rose nearly 3% in the extended session Monday after the cruise operator said that the week of March 28 was its busiest booking seven-day period ever. The week showed a "double-digit" increase from the previous record 7-day booking total, the company said, without further details.
